Turning point date, reinforced by daily turning point on same day, aligning with weekly cycle of week ending March 6

seasonal: March tends to show some weakness for Bitcoin. In 8 out of the last 15 years, Bitcoin finished March negatively (53%). However, Bitcoin's limited history (15 years) makes seasonal statistics less reliable compared to other assets in the report.

statistics.description: Over the last 9 years, Bitcoin tended to trade at a higher price on April 2 than on March 10 in 6 out of 9 years (~67%)

insights: Bitcoin has broken below the critical support band of $70,000-$78,000 which was identified as the central support zone - it has spent the entire month of February settling below this level

Bitcoin has a recurring pattern of 35 months of gains followed by 14 months of decline - the 35-month rally wave ended precisely in October 2025, and since then Bitcoin has declined ~52% from the peak
From a long-term perspective, this break of the $70,000-$78,000 support has substantial significance - it is not merely temporary weakness but potentially a structural change in the primary trend

In simple terms, Bitcoin is no longer behaving like an asset in a primary uptrend - the implication is that any rally from here should be treated as a technical correction within a developing bearish primary trend
The overall technical pattern forming is a symmetrical triangle - in most cases this suggests the natural assumption is that pressure to continue the prior direction (downward) remains present
If we see a breakout above the resistance zones, it could open the door for a rally to the $74,570 area (Fibonacci 38.2% of the entire decline), which is the probable ceiling for any upside move
Two prominent weekly cycles stand out: weeks ending March 6 and March 27 - these are the key time windows for potential turning points
The window between March 15-18 features daily turning point dates that hint at higher volatility possibilities
Toward month-end, additional convergence occurs as dates March 26-27 meet the weekly cycle - this is a central time zone to watch closely
Crypto market is characterized by high dynamics and rapid volatility - real-time decisions are essential
Not enough to just identify direction - must also pay attention to the pace at which the market moves

support.description: Current resistance/support band on daily chart - Bitcoin is struggling around this zone
Fibonacci 0% level (full retracement target) - bottom of the major decline from all-time highs
If Bitcoin reaches this range, significant support exists; if it does not hold, additional downside toward $49,000 is possible
Next support if deeper decline materializes

resistance.description: Fibonacci 38.2% retracement of the decline - most probable ceiling for any upside rally

technical.indicators: Daily chart has returned to a bearish structure with clear arrangement of lower highs and lower lows. From early February, a stabilization attempt began around the $69,900-$71,560 zone. The MACD remained below the action line throughout the last rally wave, signaling weakness. RSI shows minor negative divergence. The 20-week moving average has been supporting declines but the overall structure remains bearish.

summary: Bitcoin is in a significant long-term test zone after breaking below the critical $70,000-$78,000 support band. The 35-month cycle of gains appears to have completed in October 2025, and the current decline of ~52% from peak suggests a structural shift toward a bearish primary trend. March is not expected to be a simple month - the cyclical structure hints at sensitive time points that could create reversals, accelerations, or upside at resistance levels. Two key weekly cycles (weeks ending March 6 and March 27) are the most important time windows, with mid-month dates (March 16-17) offering higher volatility potential.
previous_month_review: February 2026 had four turning point dates. Date 1 (Feb 3): did not work - failed to block the declining price movement and missed the low set on Feb 6. Date 2 (Feb 10): was one day late - served as the high of the movement. Date 3 (Feb 17): worked partially - the actual high arrived two days earlier. Date 4 (Feb 24): worked - served as the low of the movement.
risk_notes: Bitcoin has limited history (15 years) making seasonal analysis less reliable - prioritize technical levels and real-time updates
In a bearish primary trend structure, treat any rally as a technical correction, not the start of new highs
Stay connected to the Discord community for real-time crypto updates as conditions change rapidly
The difference between a late reaction and a precise action can be the difference in crypto trading
